developer.comment: Originally built by Codeium (founded 2021 as Exafunction by Varun Mohan and Douglas Chen). After a 2025 acqui-hire saga, Cognition (maker of Devin) acquired Windsurf's IP, brand, and product in July 2025 and now develops it.

first-release.comment: The Windsurf Editor (VS Code fork built around the Cascade agent) launched in November 2024 by Codeium. The Codeium completion product predates it (2022), but Windsurf-the-agentic-IDE is dated from its Nov 2024 release per the rebrand note in RESEARCH.md.

status.comment: Actively developed under Cognition; shipping ongoing releases including SWE-1.5/1.6 models and Devin cloud-agent integration (checked 2026-06-02).

license.comment: Closed-source product (a VS Code fork) with a free tier plus paid plans. The editor/agent itself is proprietary even though it is built on open-source VS Code.

interface.comment: Primarily a standalone agentic IDE (VS Code fork). Also ships IDE plugins (e.g. JetBrains), but those plugins provide only the autocomplete action, not the full Cascade agent.

host-ides.comment: The flagship Windsurf Editor is a standalone forked editor. A JetBrains plugin exists but is limited to autocomplete; the full agent only runs in the standalone editor.

open-source.comment: The agent/editor itself is closed-source despite being built on open-source VS Code.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Standalone editor at its core; also ships an autocomplete extension/plugin for other IDEs and integrates the Devin cloud agent.

default-model-provider.comment: Offers first-class support for every major provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, plus DeepSeek, xAI, Moonshot) and ships its own SWE-1.x family as the recommended default. No single external default provider.

model-choice.comment: Users select among dozens of models from multiple providers via the model dropdown.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK is supported: BYOK models are labeled in the dropdown and users add their own API key in subscription settings.

local-model-support.comment: No native local/self-hosted model endpoint in the editor. Workarounds (running Ollama separately and copy-pasting, or exposing LM Studio as an OpenAI-compatible server via MCP) exist but are not a supported in-agent local-model feature, so marked false.

multi-file-editing.comment: Cascade plans and applies coordinated edits across multiple files, creating, modifying, and deleting files in one task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Cascade chains steps: plans actions, edits files, runs commands, reads output, installs packages, runs tests, and iteratively fixes errors toward a goal.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Cascade can run terminal commands, detect packages to install, and monitor output.

mcp-support.comment: Cascade natively integrates with the Model Context Protocol; users can add MCP servers and Cascade uses their tools, resources, and prompts.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: A specialized planning agent runs alongside the acting model, and the integrated Devin cloud agent can run delegated tasks in parallel while the developer keeps coding locally.

codebase-indexing.comment: Windsurf builds an AI-aware semantic index (Riptide) of the repository for fast retrieval across large codebases, rather than only reading files ad hoc.

planning-mode.comment: Cascade has built-in planning: a specialized planning agent continuously refines a long-term plan and generates todo lists for complex tasks.

multimodal-input.comment: Cascade accepts image input: users can drag/paste or add an image (e.g. Figma designs, wireframes, screenshots). Available with vision-capable models such as GPT-4o and Claude (the image-upload doc URL 404'd on direct fetch; confirmed via Windsurf docs search snippet).

browser-computer-use.comment: Windsurf Previews renders the website live in the IDE; the user can click any element and Cascade reshapes it, letting the agent act on and verify the rendered UI.

operating-mode.comment: Spans Tab inline completion, Cascade chat, supervised/autonomous agent runs with human-in-the-loop approval, and async delegation to the integrated Devin cloud agent.

inline-completion.comment: Provides Tab generative autocomplete as you type (unlimited across all plans including Free).

background-async-execution.comment: Devin is built into Windsurf as an autonomous cloud agent: tasks (debugging, testing, deployment) can be delegated and run while the developer keeps coding locally.

persistent-memory.comment: Cascade accumulates Memories tailored to the project and the user's style across sessions; users can save Q&As as memories and refine rules.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Cascade supports named snapshots of project state and reverting to them (reverts are irreversible).

git-integration.comment: Built on VS Code's native git tooling; Cascade can run git commands in the terminal and integrate a GitHub MCP server for PR/repo interactions. Spaces bundle PRs and files around a task.

pr-creation.comment: Via the GitHub MCP server and Cascade workflows, the agent can interact with and open/update pull requests; Spaces explicitly bundle PRs. Not a one-click native PR button outside these integrations.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project-level rules (.windsurf/rules/, formerly .windsurfrules) committed to git to encode team conventions, analogous to .cursorrules / AGENTS.md.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via reusable Workflows/Skills, custom slash commands, and MCP servers for custom tools and data sources.

permission-model.comment: Human-in-the-loop approval by default for agent actions; auto-execution / turbo behavior is configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Enterprise offers cloud, hybrid, and self-hosted deployment options, with the ability to keep all inference traffic inside customer-owned environments. Self-host is an enterprise tier, not available on consumer plans.

privacy-mode.comment: Zero data retention by default for all paid (team/enterprise) seats; inputs/outputs are not retained or used for training, and code-storing features require explicit opt-in.

enterprise-controls.comment: Enterprise includes SSO/SCIM, RBAC, audit logs, and self-hosted/hybrid deployment keeping traffic in the customer's private tenant (VPC-equivalent). SOC 2 Type II and FedRAMP High accredited.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier, monthly subscriptions (Pro/Teams/Max), plus extra usage billed at API price beyond plan quotas. Pricing overhauled to daily/weekly quotas in March 2026 (checked 2026-06-02).

free-tier.comment: Free plan at $0/mo with unlimited Tab autocomplete plus light Cascade/premium-model quota.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pro is $20/mo (raised from $15 in the March 2026 pricing overhaul). Cheapest paid plan; ~50% student discount available (checked 2026-06-02).

maturity.comment: GA, polished, and widely deployed since the Nov 2024 editor launch, but knocked from 5 by a turbulent 2025 ownership change (Codeium to Cognition) and a March 2026 pricing/model overhaul still settling.

community-size.comment: Among the larger AI coding tools (RESEARCH.md places Windsurf at the 'large' tier, below Copilot/Cursor 'huge').

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Supports an MCP server ecosystem plus shareable Workflows/Skills and rules; built on VS Code so it also leverages that extension base.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (root always-on, subdirectories auto-globbed) and vendor-specific rule files in .windsurf/rules/*.md (the legacy single .windsurfrules is the prior format) — the latter mapped to 'custom'. Also reads a global_rules.md from user config (checked 2026-06-02).

memory-scopes.comment: All four levels: enterprise/system rules in OS dirs deployed read-only by IT; user/global rules in ~/.codeium/windsurf/memories/global_rules.md; project rules in .windsurf/rules/; folder-level via AGENTS.md in subdirectories.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Each workspace rule declares a trigger: 'Always On' (always in system prompt), 'Glob' (path-based, applied when Cascade reads/edits a matching file), 'Model Decision' and 'Manual' @rule-name (agent-requested / on-demand). No separate language/file-type mechanism beyond glob, so 'file-type-based' omitted.

auto-memory.comment: Cascade automatically generates and stores cross-session memories when it judges context worth remembering. A global toggle to disable auto-generation is not documented — individual memories can be edited/deleted via the Memories panel, so 'can-be-disabled' is not asserted (checked 2026-06-02).

skill-support.comment: Supports two on-demand packaged-capability mechanisms beyond plain rules: Workflows (markdown step playbooks invoked via /slash-command) and Skills (folders bundling SKILL.md plus reference scripts/templates/checklists that Cascade invokes on demand).

skill-invocation.comment: Skills are both: Cascade autonomously invokes a skill when the request matches its description (model-callable), and the user can force one with @skill-name (user-callable). Workflows are user-callable only via /slash-command — 'Cascade will never invoke a workflow automatically'.

always-on-surface.comment: Skills use progressive disclosure: only each skill's name and description are shown to the model by default; the full SKILL.md body and supporting files load only when Cascade invokes the skill or the user mentions it.

skill-distribution.comment: No marketplace or registry documented. Skills/Workflows are shared by committing them to the repo (.windsurf/skills/, .windsurf/workflows/) for version-controlled git distribution, or placed manually in global/system directories.
